Aerospace Apr 1, 2026

Virgin Galactic expects commercial suborbital flights to resume late this year

Virgin Galactic still expects to resume commercial suborbital flights by the end of the year as its first next-generation spaceplane nears completion.

Virgin Galactic's timeline for resuming commercial operations depends on completing its next-generation spaceplane, signaling a transition from its current fleet to enhanced vehicle capabilities.

A closer look at how United Airlines is transforming one of passengers' less-favored jets.

United's repurposing of 50-seat aircraft addresses both fleet optimization and capacity utilization challenges on underperforming regional routes.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

How Much Does It Cost To Operate A Boeing 737?

Operating cost is fundamentally variable, but is typically a couple thousand dollars per hour.

737 operating costs of $2,000-3,000 per hour translate to $10-15 million annually for typical single-aircraft operators, making fleet utilization critical to profitability.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Apr 1, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway incursion hazards at high-traffic airports like LAX require pilots to understand specific exit procedures to prevent surface safety incidents during critical ground operations.

US airport security lines return to normal as TSA workers are paid

Airport security lines eased at several major US airports on March 31, 2026, after Transportation Security Administration (TSA)…...

TSA staffing disruptions demonstrate the operational vulnerability of US airports to federal workforce disruptions, with immediate ripple effects on passenger throughput and airline schedules.

Industry Apr 1, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and airspace—rather than aircraft redesign alone—offers the fastest pathway to meet dual capacity and environmental demands as traffic grows.

See MDPI →

EASA: Narrow Mideast Corridors Increase Operational Risk

European regulators say conflict-related airspace restrictions are increasing congestion and risks.

Compressed routing through conflict zones forces aircraft into tighter flight corridors, amplifying collision risk and reducing pilot reaction time despite overall airspace closures.
